name: UK Data Protection Act 2018
summary: The UK Data Protection Act 2018 supplements the UK GDPR (retained EU law post-Brexit) and provides a comprehensive data protection framework. The Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) is the independent supervisory authority responsible for enforcement.
keyRequirements: Lawful basis required for processing personal data (mirrors UK GDPR)
Enhanced protections for special category and criminal conviction data
Rights of data subjects including access, rectification, and erasure
Data Protection Officer required for public authorities and high-risk processing
International data transfer mechanisms (UK adequacy regulations, SCCs)
Mandatory breach notification to ICO within 72 hours
Age-appropriate design code for children's online services
penalties: Up to GBP 17.5 million or 4% of annual global turnover, whichever is greater.
